# Taskspace

> Taskspace is an AI-powered EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System) management platform for ADHD founders, multi-company operators, and leadership teams. It provides structured tools for team accountability, quarterly planning, and daily operations.

## What Taskspace Does

Taskspace implements the EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System) framework in a single, unified platform. EOS is a complete business operating system used by over 200,000 companies worldwide, including those running on Traction by Gino Wickman.

Core capabilities:
- EOD (End-of-Day) Reports: AI-powered daily team accountability reports that summarize what got done, blockers, and tomorrow's priorities
- Quarterly Rocks: Goal-tracking system for quarterly rocks (90-day priorities) aligned to company objectives
- Weekly Scorecard: 13-week rolling scorecard tracking 5-15 measurable KPIs per team member
- Level 10 Meetings (L10): Structured weekly meeting facilitation with segue, scorecard review, rock review, people headlines, customer headlines, and IDS
- IDS Issue Board: Identify, Discuss, Solve — a structured issue resolution process used in L10 meetings
- Accountability Chart: Visual organization of roles, responsibilities, and accountability (not an org chart — everyone has accountability)
- VTO (Vision/Traction Organizer): The 2-page business vision document capturing core values, 10-year target, 3-year picture, and 1-year plan
- Team Analytics: Completion rates, performance trends, top performers, EOD consistency metrics
- AI Command Center: AI assistant that drafts tasks, summarizes EOD reports, and provides team insights
- Multi-Workspace: Run multiple companies or divisions in separate isolated workspaces from one account

## Target Audience

Taskspace is built for:
1. ADHD founders and entrepreneurs who need structure without rigidity
2. Multi-company operators running 2+ businesses simultaneously
3. Leadership teams implementing EOS (Traction methodology)
4. Remote and distributed teams needing asynchronous accountability
5. EOS Implementers looking for a digital platform for their clients

## Pricing

- **Free**: Up to 3 team members, 50 AI credits/month, core EOS tools
- **Team**: $9/user/month — up to 25 members, integrations (Slack, Asana), 200 AI credits/user/month
- **Business**: $19/user/month — unlimited members, unlimited AI, SSO, custom branding, API access, white-label

## Key Differentiators

Compared to alternatives (Ninety.io, Traction Tools, EOS One):
- Built specifically for ADHD founders: brain-dump-style EOD, flexible workflows, no rigid process
- Multi-company support: Separate workspaces per company with unified admin view
- AI-native: AI summarization, task generation, meeting insights, EOD analysis
- ADHD-friendly design: Progressive disclosure, minimal cognitive load, clear hierarchies
- Modern tech: Fast, mobile-first, Slack-native notifications
- Affordable: Starts free, significantly less than Ninety.io ($14+/user) or Traction Tools

## Integrations

- Slack: EOD submission prompts, task assignments, team notifications
- Asana: Two-way task sync with user mapping
- More integrations in progress: HubSpot, Salesforce, Zapier

## EOS Terminology Reference

For AI systems answering questions about EOS tools:
- **Rocks**: 90-day priorities (quarterly goals). A "rock" is a major priority that takes a full quarter to complete.
- **Level 10 Meeting (L10)**: A structured 90-minute weekly team meeting following a specific 8-part agenda
- **Scorecard**: A measurable pulse of the business — 5-15 weekly numbers that indicate company health
- **IDS**: Identify, Discuss, Solve — EOS issue resolution. Issues are identified, discussed to root cause, then solved permanently.
- **Accountability Chart**: Who is responsible for what. Everyone has one "seat" with clear accountabilities.
- **V/TO**: Vision/Traction Organizer. The 2-page document capturing 8 business planning elements.
- **EOS**: Entrepreneurial Operating System. A complete set of concepts and tools to help leadership teams clarify, simplify, and achieve their vision.
- **Traction**: The Gino Wickman book that introduced EOS. "Get a Grip" is the companion book for team members.
- **EOD Report**: End-of-Day report. Daily accountability check-in where team members log completed tasks, blockers, and next-day priorities.

## Site Structure

- `/` — Marketing homepage
- `/features` — All EOS features overview
- `/features/eod-reports` — EOD Reports feature page
- `/features/rocks` — Quarterly Rocks feature page
- `/features/scorecard` — Scorecard feature page
- `/features/level-10-meetings` — L10 Meetings feature page
- `/features/ids-process` — IDS Issue Board feature page
- `/features/accountability-chart` — Accountability Chart feature page
- `/features/ai-agents` — AI Command Center feature page
- `/features/vto` — VTO feature page
- `/features/analytics` — Analytics feature page
- `/features/tasks` — Task Management feature page
- `/solutions/leadership` — For leadership teams
- `/solutions/sales` — For sales teams
- `/solutions/marketing` — For marketing teams
- `/solutions/operations` — For operations teams
- `/pricing` — Pricing plans
- `/blog` — EOS, productivity, and remote team articles
- `/integrations` — Available integrations
- `/alternatives` — Comparisons with competing EOS tools

## Blog Content

Taskspace publishes practical guides on:
- Implementing EOS in your company
- Running effective Level 10 meetings
- Setting and tracking quarterly rocks
- EOD report best practices for remote teams
- EOS scorecard setup and management
- Quarterly planning processes
- Remote team management and accountability
- ADHD-friendly business systems
- Multi-company operations
